creator: zirias@freebsd.org
text:
Well, a first test build succeeds!

Still there are a few missing dependencies:

- LIB_DEPENDS also needs libgraphene-1.0.so:graphics/graphene, libharfbuzz.so:print/harfbuzz and libvulkan.so:graphics/vulkan-loader
- USE_GNOME also needs cairo

The build works because they are pulled in by other dependencies, but as they are also *direct* dependencies, they must be named.

Then for the stylistic topics:

- COMMENT belongs into the MAINTAINER section, put it directly after the MAINTAINER line. (The Makefile should start with PORTNAME)
- This section also needs a WWW variable (after COMMENT), which should contain the human-readable homepage of the project. In your case probably the same as MASTER_SITES, but that's just coincidence.
- Avoid multiple blank lines and unnecessary whitespace at the end of lines (one line in the Makefile containing only whitespace must be removed, and an extra whitespace in pkg-descr must be removed).
- When PORTVERSION and DISTVERSION are the same, it's preferred to set DISTVERSION.
- The USES section must be separated from the *_DEPENDS section by a blank line.
- Your port only installs two files. Therefore consider removing the pkg-plist file and instead name the files in the PLIST_FILES variable directly in the port Makefile

Apart from that, when you improve your upstream Makefile so it doesn't need patching, please also change your install: target to respect $(DESTDIR). Just prepend it to every path and don't define it (so it's empty if not specified by the user). This is common practice and will help packaging your software for many systems. Then, the do-install: target from the port Makefile won't be needed any more.

----
General tips:

- Use portlint and portclippy (from portfmt package) to check your port for stylistic issues. They're sometimes wrong of course, but in general quite helpful.

- To check your dependencies, running 'make stage-qa' is helpful.

- IMHO even better, install poudriere and run 'poudriere testport' -- it will automatically call stage-qa and can be configured to include portlint, and as it builds in a clean environment, you can be *very* sure to catch all dependency issues.

----

Please don't be shocked by that long list. As I said on the forums, the port as submitted looks pretty sane. Getting a port into "perfect shape" takes some experience ;)
